definitions: An optical illusion that exaggerates the contrast between edges of the slightly differing shades of gray as soon as they touch by making the lighter area seem lighter and the darker area seem darker.
A visual phenomenon where the human visual system perceives a brighter or darker band at the boundary of two regions with different luminances, even if the luminance gradient is smooth.
examples: The Mach band effect is a classic example of lateral inhibition in the visual system.
Artists sometimes use the principle of the Mach band to create visual depth.
Understanding the Mach band helps in image processing and display calibration.
